contentBlocks.original:
॥ शान्ति पाठ ॥

ॐ भद्रं कर्णेभिः श‍ृणुयाम देवा ।
भद्रं पश्येमाक्षभिर्यजत्राः ॥
स्थिरैरङ्गैस्तुष्टुवाग्‍ँसस्तनूभिः ।
व्यशेम देवहितं यदायुः ॥
ॐ स्वस्ति न इन्द्रो वृद्धश्रवाः ।
स्वस्ति नः पूषा विश्ववेदाः ॥
स्वस्तिनस्तार्क्ष्यो अरिष्टनेमिः ।
स्वस्ति नो बृहस्पतिर्दधातु ॥
ॐ शांतिः । शांतिः ॥ शांतिः॥।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śānti pāṭha
oṃ bhadraṃ karṇebhiḥ śṛṇuyāma devā .
bhadraṃ paśyemākṣabhiryajatrāḥ .
sthirairaṅgaistuṣṭuvāgṃ sastanūbhiḥ .
vyaśema devahitaṃ yadāyuḥ .
oṃ svasti na indro vṛddhaśravāḥ .
svasti naḥ pūṣā viśvavedāḥ .
svastinastārkṣyo ariṣṭanemiḥ .
svasti no bṛhaspatirdadhātu .
oṃ śāntiḥ śāntiḥ śāntiḥ ..
contentBlocks.translation: O Deities! By ears may we hear what is auspicious; by eyes may we see what is auspicious; O Worshipped ones! With hale and hearty limbs may we live offering our praises (unto Thee). For the benefit of God may we live the entire span of life. May the ancient and famous Indra give blessings to us. May the all-knowing Sun give blessings to us. May the Lord of swift motion (Garuda), who removes our obstacles give blessings to us. May Brhaspati (Guru of deities) give blessings to us. Let there be peace, let there be peace, let there be peace.
contentBlocks.meanings: ॐ (oṃ) – sacred sound; भद्रं (bhadraṃ) – auspicious; कर्णेभिः (karṇebhiḥ) – by ears; श‍ृणुयाम (śṛṇuyāma) – may we hear; देवाः (devāḥ) – O deities; भद्रं (bhadraṃ) – auspicious; पश्येम (paśyema) – may we see; अक्षभिः (akṣabhiḥ) – by eyes; यजत्राः (yajatrāḥ) – O worshipped ones; स्थिरैः (sthiraiḥ) – with firm; अङ्गैः (aṅgaiḥ) – limbs; तुष्टुवाग्‍ँसः (tuṣṭuvāgṃsaḥ) – praising; तनूभिः (tanūbhiḥ) – with bodies; व्यशेम (vyaśema) – may we live; देवहितं (devahitaṃ) – for the benefit of God (deva:God | hitaṃ:benefit); यदायुः (yadāyuḥ) – entire span of life (yat:entire | āyuḥ:life); ॐ (oṃ) – sacred sound; स्वस्ति (svasti) – blessings; न (na) – to us; इन्द्रः (indraḥ) – Indra; वृद्धश्रवाः (vṛddhaśravāḥ) – ancient and famous (vṛddha:ancient | śravāḥ:famous); स्वस्ति (svasti) – blessings; नः (naḥ) – to us; पूषा (pūṣā) – Sun; विश्ववेदाः (viśvavedāḥ) – all-knowing (viśva:all | vedāḥ:knowing); स्वस्ति (svasti) – blessings; नः (naḥ) – to us; तार्क्ष्यः (tārkṣyaḥ) – Garuda; अरिष्टनेमिः (ariṣṭanemiḥ) – remover of obstacles (ariṣṭa:obstacles | nemiḥ:remover); स्वस्ति (svasti) – blessings; नः (naḥ) – to us; बृहस्पतिः (bṛhaspatiḥ) – Bṛhaspati; दधातु (dadhātu) – may give; ॐ (oṃ) – sacred sound; शांतिः (śāntiḥ) – peace; शांतिः (śāntiḥ) – peace; शांतिः (śāntiḥ) – peace;

contentBlocks.original:
॥ उपनिषत् ॥

हरिः ॐ नमस्ते गणपतये ॥
त्वमेव प्रत्यक्षं तत्त्वमसि ॥
त्वमेव केवलं कर्ताऽसि ॥
त्वमेव केवलं धर्ताऽसि ॥
त्वमेव केवलं हर्ताऽसि ॥
त्वमेव सर्वं खल्विदं ब्रह्मासि ॥
त्वं साक्षादात्माऽसि नित्यम् ॥१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
upaniṣat
hariḥ oṃ namaste gaṇapataye .
tvameva pratyakṣaṃ tattvamasi .
tvameva kevalaṃ kartā'si .
tvameva kevalaṃ dhartā'si .
tvameva kevalaṃ hartā'si .
tvameva sarvaṃ khalvidaṃ brahmāsi .
tvaṃ sākṣādātmā'si nityam. 1
contentBlocks.translation: Om. Prostration to Lord Ganapati who is of the nature of OM. You alone are the direct essence of the statement 'That thou art'. You alone indeed are the creator, sustainer and destroyer. You alone as Brahman are indeed all this. You are the directly experienced Self which is eternal.
contentBlocks.meanings: हरिः (hariḥ) – Hari; ॐ (oṃ) – Om; नमस्ते (namaste) – salutations; गणपतये (gaṇapataye) – to Ganapati; त्वम् (tvam) – you; एव (eva) – indeed; प्रत्यक्षं (pratyakṣam) – directly; तत्त्वमसि (tattvamasi) – you are that (tat:that | tvam:you | asi:are); त्वम् (tvam) – you; एव (eva) – indeed; केवलं (kevalaṃ) – alone; कर्ताऽसि (kartā'si) – you are the creator (kartā:creator | asi:are); त्वम् (tvam) – you; एव (eva) – indeed; केवलं (kevalaṃ) – alone; धर्ताऽसि (dhartā'si) – you are the sustainer (dhartā:sustainer | asi:are); त्वम् (tvam) – you; एव (eva) – indeed; केवलं (kevalaṃ) – alone; हर्ताऽसि (hartā'si) – you are the destroyer (hartā:destroyer | asi:are); त्वम् (tvam) – you; एव (eva) – indeed; सर्वं (sarvaṃ) – all; खलु (khalu) – indeed; इदं (idaṃ) – this; ब्रह्मासि (brahmāsi) – you are Brahman (brahma:Brahman | asi:are); त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; साक्षात् (sākṣāt) – directly; आत्माऽसि (ātmā'si) – you are the Self (ātmā:Self | asi:are); नित्यम् (nityam) – eternal;

contentBlocks.original:
॥ स्वरूप तत्त्व ॥

ऋतं वच्मि (वदिष्यामि) ॥ सत्यं वच्मि (वदिष्यामि) ॥ २॥
अव त्वं माम् ॥ अव वक्तारम् ॥ अव श्रोतारम् ॥
अव दातारम् ॥ अव धातारम् ॥
अवानूचानमव शिष्यम् ॥
अव पश्चात्तात् ॥ अव पुरस्तात् ॥
अवोत्तरात्तात् ॥ अव दक्षिणात्तात् ॥
अव चोर्ध्वात्तात् ॥ अवाधरात्तात् ॥
सर्वतो मां पाहि पाहि समंतात् ॥ ३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
svarūpa tattva
ṛtaṃ vacmi (vadiṣyāmi) . satyaṃ vacmi (vadiṣyāmi) 2

ava tvaṃ mām . ava vaktāram . ava śrotāram .
ava dātāram . ava dhātāram .
avānūcānamava śiṣyam .
ava paścāttāt . ava purastāt .
avottarāttāt . ava dakṣiṇāttāt .
ava cordhvāttāt . avādharāttāt .
sarvato māṃ pāhi pāhi samantāt. 3
contentBlocks.translation:
I speak the Truth (as determined by the scriptures). I speak the Truth (as directly experienced and verified by myself).

You please protect me. Please protect the speaker, the listener, the donor and the supporter. Please protect the teacher and the disciple. Please protect me from all four directions (east, west, north and south). Please protect me from the above and below. Please protect me totally from all directions.
contentBlocks.meanings: स्वरूप (svarūpa) – true nature; तत्त्व (tattva) – essence; ऋतं (ṛtaṃ) – truth; वच्मि (vacmi) – I speak; सत्यं (satyaṃ) – truth; वच्मि (vacmi) – I speak; अव (ava) – protect; त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; माम् (mām) – me; अव (ava) – protect; वक्तारम् (vaktāram) – speaker; अव (ava) – protect; श्रोतारम् (śrotāram) – listener; अव (ava) – protect; दातारम् (dātāram) – donor; अव (ava) – protect; धातारम् (dhātāram) – supporter; अवानूचानम् (avānūcānam) – protect the teacher (ava:protect | anūcānam:teacher); अव (ava) – protect; शिष्यम् (śiṣyam) – disciple; अव (ava) – protect; पश्चात्तात् (paścāttāt) – from the west; अव (ava) – protect; पुरस्तात् (purastāt) – from the east; अवोत्तरात्तात् (avottarāttāt) – protect from the north (ava:protect | uttarāttāt:from the north); अव (ava) – protect; दक्षिणात्तात् (dakṣiṇāttāt) – from the south; अव (ava) – protect; चोर्ध्वात्तात् (cordhvāttāt) – from above (ca:and | ūrdhvāttāt:from above); अव (ava) – protect; अधरात्तात् (adharāttāt) – from below; सर्वतो (sarvato) – from all directions; मां (māṃ) – me; पाहि (pāhi) – protect; पाहि (pāhi) – protect; समंतात् (samantāt) – totally;

contentBlocks.original:
त्वं वाङ्मयस्त्वं चिन्मयः ॥
त्वमानंदमयस्त्वं ब्रह्ममयः ॥
त्वं सच्चिदानंदाद्वितीयोऽसि ॥
त्वं प्रत्यक्षं ब्रह्मासि ॥
त्वं ज्ञानमयो विज्ञानमयोऽसि ॥ ४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvaṃ vāṅmayastvaṃ cinmayaḥ .
tvamānandamayastvaṃ brahmamayaḥ .
tvaṃ saccidānandādvitīyo'si .
tvaṃ pratyakṣaṃ brahmāsi .
tvaṃ jñānamayo vijñānamayo'si. 4
contentBlocks.translation: You are the embodiment of speech. You are the embodiment of Consciousness. You are the embodiment of Bliss. You are the embodiment of infinitude. You are non-dual Existence-Consciousness Bliss. You are the self-evident Brahman/Reality. You are the embodiment of knowledge and wisdom.
contentBlocks.meanings: त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; वाङ्मयः (vāṅmayaḥ) – embodiment of speech (vāk:speech | mayaḥ:embodiment of); त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; चिन्मयः (cinmayaḥ) – embodiment of Consciousness (cit:Consciousness | mayaḥ:embodiment of); त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; आनंदमयः (ānandamayaḥ) – embodiment of Bliss (ānanda:Bliss | mayaḥ:embodiment of); त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; ब्रह्ममयः (brahmamayaḥ) – embodiment of Brahman (brahma:Brahman | mayaḥ:embodiment of); त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; सच्चिदानंदाद्वितीयः (saccidānandādvitīyaḥ) – non-dual Existence-Consciousness-Bliss (sat:Existence | cit:Consciousness | ānanda:Bliss | advitīyaḥ:non-dual); असि (asi) – are; त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; प्रत्यक्षं (pratyakṣam) – self-evident; ब्रह्म (brahma) – Brahman; असि (asi) – are; त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; ज्ञानमयः (jñānamayaḥ) – embodiment of knowledge (jñāna:knowledge | mayaḥ:embodiment of); विज्ञानमयः (vijñānamayaḥ) – embodiment of wisdom (vijñāna:wisdom | mayaḥ:embodiment of); असि (asi) – are;

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वं जगदिदं त्वत्तो जायते ॥
सर्वं जगदिदं त्वत्तस्तिष्ठति ॥
सर्वं जगदिदं त्वयि लयमेष्यति ॥
सर्वं जगदिदं त्वयि प्रत्येति ॥
त्वं भूमिरापोऽनलोऽनिलो नभः ॥
त्वं चत्वारि वाक्पदानि ॥ ५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvaṃ jagadidaṃ tvatto jāyate .
sarvaṃ jagadidaṃ tvattastiṣṭhati .
sarvaṃ jagadidaṃ tvayi layameṣyati .
sarvaṃ jagadidaṃ tvayi pratyeti .
tvaṃ bhūmirāpo'nalo'nilo nabhaḥ .
tvaṃ catvāri vākpadāni. 5
contentBlocks.translation: This entire world is born from You. This entire world exists because of You. This entire world merges in You. This entire world appears in You. You are the earth, water, fire, air and space (the world of matter). You are the four kinds of speech (the world of speech).
contentBlocks.meanings: सर्वं (sarvaṃ) – all; जगत् (jagat) – world; इदं (idaṃ) – this; त्वत्तः (tvattaḥ) – from You; जायते (jāyate) – is born; सर्वं (sarvaṃ) – all; जगत् (jagat) – world; इदं (idaṃ) – this; त्वत्तः (tvattaḥ) – because of You; तिष्ठति (tiṣṭhati) – exists; सर्वं (sarvaṃ) – all; जगत् (jagat) – world; इदं (idaṃ) – this; त्वयि (tvayi) – in You; लयम् (layam) – merges; एष्यति (eṣyati) – will attain; सर्वं (sarvaṃ) – all; जगत् (jagat) – world; इदं (idaṃ) – this; त्वयि (tvayi) – in You; प्रत्येति (pratyeti) – appears; त्वं (tvaṃ) – You; भूमिः (bhūmiḥ) – earth; आपः (āpaḥ) – water; अनलः (analaḥ) – fire; अनिलः (anilaḥ) – air; नभः (nabhaḥ) – space; त्वं (tvaṃ) – You; चत्वारि (catvāri) – four; वाक् (vāk) – speech; पदानि (padāni) – words;

contentBlocks.original:
त्वं गुणत्रयातीतः त्वमवस्थात्रयातीतः ॥
त्वं देहत्रयातीतः ॥ त्वं कालत्रयातीतः ॥
त्वं मूलाधारस्थितोऽसि नित्यम् ॥
त्वं शक्तित्रयात्मकः ॥
त्वां योगिनो ध्यायंति नित्यम् ॥
त्वं ब्रह्मा त्वं विष्णुस्त्वं रुद्रस्त्वं
इन्द्रस्त्वं अग्निस्त्वं वायुस्त्वं सूर्यस्त्वं चंद्रमास्त्वं
ब्रह्मभूर्भुवःस्वरोम् ॥ ६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvaṃ guṇatrayātītaḥ tvamavasthātrayātītaḥ .
tvaṃ dehatrayātītaḥ . tvaṃ kālatrayātītaḥ .
tvaṃ mūlādhārasthito'si nityam .
tvaṃ śaktitrayātmakaḥ .
tvāṃ yogino dhyāyanti nityam .
tvaṃ brahmā tvaṃ viṣṇustvaṃ rudrastvaṃ
indrastvaṃ agnistvaṃ vāyustvaṃ sūryastvaṃ caṃdramāstvaṃ
brahmabhūrbhuvaḥsvarom. 6
contentBlocks.translation:
You are beyond the three Gunas (qualities). You are beyond the three states of experience. You are beyond the three bodies. You are beyond the three periods of time.
You are ever seated in the base (mūlādhāra). You are of the nature of the three-fold power. Yogis ever meditate on You.
You are Brahmā, Visnu, Rudra, Indra, Fire, Air, Sun, Moon (divine deities). You are Brahman/Truth. You are the three worlds.
contentBlocks.meanings: त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; गुणत्रयातीतः (guṇatrayātītaḥ) – beyond the three Gunas (guṇa:quality | traya:three | atītaḥ:beyond); त्वम् (tvam) – you; अवस्थात्रयातीतः (avasthātrayātītaḥ) – beyond the three states (avasthā:state | traya:three | atītaḥ:beyond); त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; देहत्रयातीतः (dehatrayātītaḥ) – beyond the three bodies (deha:body | traya:three | atītaḥ:beyond); त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; कालत्रयातीतः (kālatrayātītaḥ) – beyond the three times (kāla:time | traya:three | atītaḥ:beyond); त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; मूलाधारस्थितोऽसि (mūlādhārasthito'si) – seated in the base (mūlādhāra:base | sthitaḥ:seated | asi:are); नित्यम् (nityam) – always; त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; शक्तित्रयात्मकः (śaktitrayātmakaḥ) – of the nature of three powers (śakti:power | traya:three | ātmakaḥ:nature of); त्वां (tvāṃ) – you; योगिनः (yoginaḥ) – yogis; ध्यायंति (dhyāyanti) – meditate; नित्यम् (nityam) – always; त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; ब्रह्मा (brahmā) – Brahmā; त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; विष्णुः (viṣṇuḥ) – Viṣṇu; त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; रुद्रः (rudraḥ) – Rudra; त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; इन्द्रः (indraḥ) – Indra; त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; अग्निः (agniḥ) – Fire; त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; वायुः (vāyuḥ) – Air; त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; सूर्यः (sūryaḥ) – Sun; त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; चंद्रमाः (candramaḥ) – Moon; त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; ब्रह्म (brahma) – Brahman/Truth; भूः (bhūḥ) – earth; भुवः (bhuvaḥ) – atmosphere; स्वः (svaḥ) – heaven; ॐ (om) – Om;

contentBlocks.original:
॥ गणेश मंत्र ॥

गणादिं पूर्वमुच्चार्य वर्णादिं तदनंतरम् ॥
अनुस्वारः परतरः ॥ अर्धेन्दुलसितम् ॥ तारेण ऋद्धम् ॥
एतत्तव मनुस्वरूपम् ॥ गकारः पूर्वरूपम् ॥
अकारो मध्यमरूपम् ॥ अनुस्वारश्चान्त्यरूपम् ॥
बिन्दुरुत्तररूपम् ॥ नादः संधानम् ॥
संहितासंधिः ॥
सैषा गणेशविद्या ॥ गणकऋषिः ॥ निचृद्गायत्रीच्छंदः ॥
गणपतिर्देवता ॥
ॐ गं गणपतये नमः ॥ ७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
Gaṇeśa mantra
gaṇādiṃ pūrvamuccārya varṇādiṃ tadanaṃtaram .
anusvāraḥ parataraḥ . ardhendulasitam . tāreṇa ṛddham .
etattava manusvarūpam . gakāraḥ pūrvarūpam .
akāro madhyamarūpam . anusvāraścāntyarūpam .
binduruttararūpam . nādaḥ sandhānam .
saṃhitāsandhiḥ .
saiṣā gaṇeśavidyā . gaṇakaṛṣiḥ . nicṛdgāyatrīcchandaḥ .
gaṇapatirdevatā .
oṃ gaṃ gaṇapataye namaḥ. 7
contentBlocks.translation:
One should first utter the first syllable ‘ga’ in the word ‘gana’ followed by ‘a’ the first of alphabets, add an ‘anusvāra’ (a nasal ‘m’ indicated by a dot above the line); then adorn it by a crescent (also a nasal), prefix ‘Om’ to it. And this represents (O Lord Ganapati!) Your terrestrial form (endowed with guëas). ‘Ga’ is the first form, ‘a’ is the middle form, ‘anusvāra’ is the subsequent form and the dot ‘bindu’ – a nasal denoted by crescent – is the concluding form; the sound ‘nāda’ is the intermediary and ‘samhitā sandhi’ is the close connection (of syllable).
This is Ganeśa Vidyā. The Sage or Seer (of this mantra) is Ganaka, ‘nicrt gayatri' is its metre. Ganapati is the deity (of this mantra).
Salutation to (Lord) Ganapati!
contentBlocks.meanings: गणादिं (gaṇādiṃ) – syllable 'ga' in 'gana' (gaṇa:group | ādiṃ:beginning); पूर्वमुच्चार्य (pūrvam uccārya) – first uttering (pūrvam:first | uccārya:uttering); वर्णादिं (varṇādiṃ) – first of alphabets (varṇa:letter | ādiṃ:beginning); तदनंतरम् (tadanaṃtaram) – then after that (tad:that | anaṃtaram:after); अनुस्वारः (anusvāraḥ) – anusvāra (nasal sound); परतरः (parataraḥ) – subsequent; अर्धेन्दुलसितम् (ardhendulasitam) – adorned by crescent (ardha:half | indu:moon | lasitam:adorned); तारेण (tāreṇa) – by a star; ऋद्धम् (ṛddham) – endowed; एतत्तव (etat tava) – this is your (etat:this | tava:your); मनुस्वरूपम् (manusvarūpam) – terrestrial form (manu:human | svarūpam:form); गकारः (gakāraḥ) – 'ga' is the form; पूर्वरूपम् (pūrvarūpam) – first form (pūrva:first | rūpam:form); अकारो (akāro) – 'a' is the form; मध्यमरूपम् (madhyamarūpam) – middle form (madhyama:middle | rūpam:form); अनुस्वारश्च (anusvāraśca) – anusvāra and; अन्त्यरूपम् (antyarūpam) – subsequent form (antya:subsequent | rūpam:form); बिन्दुरुत्तररूपम् (binduruttararūpam) – dot is concluding form (bindu:dot | uttara:concluding | rūpam:form); नादः (nādaḥ) – sound; संधानम् (sandhānam) – connection; संहितासंधिः (saṃhitāsandhiḥ) – close connection (saṃhitā:close | sandhiḥ:connection); सैषा (saiṣā) – this is; गणेशविद्या (gaṇeśavidyā) – Ganesha knowledge (gaṇeśa:Ganesha | vidyā:knowledge); गणकऋषिः (gaṇakaṛṣiḥ) – Ganaka sage (gaṇaka:Ganaka | ṛṣiḥ:sage); निचृद्गायत्रीच्छंदः (nicṛdgāyatrīcchandaḥ) – nicrt Gayatri meter (nicṛt:nicrt | gāyatrī:Gayatri | chandaḥ:meter); गणपतिर्देवता (gaṇapatirdevatā) – Ganapati deity (gaṇapatiḥ:Ganapati | devatā:deity); ॐ (oṃ) – Om; गं (gaṃ) – syllable 'ga'; गणपतये (gaṇapataye) – to Ganapati; नमः (namaḥ) – salutation;

contentBlocks.original:
॥ गणेश गायत्री ॥

एकदंताय विद्महे । वक्रतुण्डाय धीमहि ॥
तन्नो दंतिः प्रचोदयात् ॥ ८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
Gaṇeśa gāyatrī
ekadaṃtāya vidmahe . vakratuṇḍāya dhīmahi .
tanno dantiḥ pracodayāt. 8
contentBlocks.translation: We have heard (know) about the one-tusked one; we meditate on the one having a twisted trunk. May that Ganapati (one-tusked) inspire us (to meditate).
contentBlocks.meanings: एकदंताय (ekadaṃtāya) – to the one-tusked (eka:one | daṃta:tusk | āya:to); विद्महे (vidmahe) – we know; वक्रतुण्डाय (vakratuṇḍāya) – to the twisted trunk (vakra:twisted | tuṇḍa:trunk | āya:to); धीमहि (dhīmahi) – we meditate; तन्नः (tannoḥ) – that to us (tat:that | naḥ:to us); दंतिः (dantiḥ) – Ganapati (one-tusked); प्रचोदयात् (pracodayāt) – may inspire;

contentBlocks.original:
॥ गणेश रूप ॥

एकदंतं चतुर्हस्तं पाशमंकुशधारिणम् ॥
रदं च वरदं हस्तैर्बिभ्राणं मूषकध्वजम् ॥
रक्तं लंबोदरं शूर्पकर्णकं रक्तवाससम् ॥
रक्तगंधानुलिप्तांगं रक्तपुष्पैः सुपूजितम् ॥
भक्तानुकंपिनं देवं जगत्कारणमच्युतम् ॥
आविर्भूतं च सृष्ट्यादौ प्रकृतेः पुरुषात्परम् ॥
एवं ध्यायति यो नित्यं स योगी योगिनां वरः ॥ ९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
Gaṇeśa rūpa
ekadantaṃ caturhastaṃ pāśamaṅkuśadhāriṇam .
radaṃ ca varadaṃ hastairbibhrāṇaṃ mūṣakadhvajam .
raktaṃ lambodaraṃ śūrpakarṇakaṃ raktavāsasam .
raktagandhānuliptāṅgaṃ raktapuṣpaiḥ supūjitam .
bhaktānukampinaṃ devaṃ jagatkāraṇamacyutam .
āvirbhūtaṃ ca sṛṣṭyādau prakṛteḥ puruṣātparam .
evaṃ dhyāyati yo nityaṃ sa yogī yogināṃ varaḥ 9
contentBlocks.translation: One who constantly meditates on Him, who has one (right) tusk, who has four hands, who holds a noose (in His upper right hand) and a goad (in the left), who holds a tusk in His (lower left) hand, who has held the fingers of His (right lower) hand in a posture bestowing blessing, whose flag bears the insignia of mouse, who is red in colour, who is big-bellied, whose ears have the form of a scuttle, who has worn red clothes, whose body is smeared with red sandal paste, who is well worshipped with red flowers, who is compassionate towards His devotees, who is self-effulgent, who is the cause of the world, who never has a fall from his nature, who manifested/existed even before creation and who is beyond prakrti and Purusha – the yogi who thus meditates on Him is the best among yogis.
contentBlocks.meanings: गणेश (gaṇeśa) – Ganesha; रूप (rūpa) – form; एकदंतं (ekadantaṃ) – one-tusked (eka:one | dantaṃ:tusked); चतुर्हस्तं (caturhastaṃ) – four-handed (catur:four | hastaṃ:handed); पाशमंकुशधारिणम् (pāśamaṅkuśadhāriṇam) – holding noose and goad (pāśa:noose | aṅkuśa:goad | dhāriṇam:holding); रदं (radaṃ) – tusk; च (ca) – and; वरदं (varadaṃ) – bestowing boons; हस्तैः (hastaiḥ) – with hands; बिभ्राणं (bibhrāṇaṃ) – holding; मूषकध्वजम् (mūṣakadhvajam) – mouse-bannered (mūṣaka:mouse | dhvajam:bannered); रक्तं (raktaṃ) – red; लंबोदरं (lambodaraṃ) – big-bellied (lamba:big | udaraṃ:bellied); शूर्पकर्णकं (śūrpakarṇakaṃ) – winnow-eared (śūrpa:winnow | karṇakaṃ:eared); रक्तवाससम् (raktavāsasam) – wearing red clothes (rakta:red | vāsasam:clothes); रक्तगंधानुलिप्तांगं (raktagandhānuliptāṅgaṃ) – smeared with red sandal (rakta:red | gandha:sandal | anulipta:smeared | aṅgaṃ:body); रक्तपुष्पैः (raktapuṣpaiḥ) – with red flowers (rakta:red | puṣpaiḥ:with flowers); सुपूजितम् (supūjitam) – well worshipped; भक्तानुकंपिनं (bhaktānukampinaṃ) – compassionate to devotees (bhakta:devotees | anukampinaṃ:compassionate); देवं (devaṃ) – god; जगत्कारणमच्युतम् (jagatkāraṇamacyutam) – world's cause, imperishable (jagat:world | kāraṇam:cause | acyutam:imperishable); आविर्भूतं (āvirbhūtaṃ) – manifested; च (ca) – and; सृष्ट्यादौ (sṛṣṭyādau) – at creation's beginning (sṛṣṭi:creation | ādau:beginning); प्रकृतेः (prakṛteḥ) – of nature; पुरुषात्परम् (puruṣātparam) – beyond Purusha (puruṣāt:from Purusha | param:beyond); एवं (evaṃ) – thus; ध्यायति (dhyāyati) – meditates; यः (yaḥ) – who; नित्यं (nityaṃ) – constantly; स (sa) – he; योगी (yogī) – yogi; योगिनां (yogināṃ) – of yogis; वरः (varaḥ) – best;

contentBlocks.original:
॥ अष्ट नाम गणपति ॥

नमो व्रातपतये । नमो गणपतये । नमः प्रमथपतये ।
नमस्तेऽस्तु लंबोदरायैकदंताय ।
विघ्ननाशिने शिवसुताय । श्रीवरदमूर्तये नमो नमः ॥ १०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aṣṭa nāma gaṇapati
namo vrātapataye . namo gaṇapataye . namaḥ pramathapataye .
namaste'stu laṃbodarāyaikadantāya .
vighnanāśine śivasutāya . śrīvaradamūrtaye namo namaḥ. 10.
contentBlocks.translation: Prostration to the Lord of all who earn through hard work (vrāta). Prostration to the Lord of all the groups (ganas). Prostration to the Lord of the special devotees of Lord Siva (pramathas). Prostration unto You who are big-bellied, one-tusked, destroyer of obstacles and the son of Siva. Prostration to the form who bestows all desires.
contentBlocks.meanings: अष्ट (aṣṭa) – eight; नाम (nāma) – name; गणपति (gaṇapati) – Ganesha; नमो (namo) – salutations; व्रातपतये (vrātapataye) – to the Lord of groups (vrāta:group | pataye:to the Lord); नमो (namo) – salutations; गणपतये (gaṇapataye) – to Ganesha; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; प्रमथपतये (pramathapataye) – to the Lord of pramathas (pramatha:pramathas | pataye:to the Lord); नमस्तेऽस्तु (namaste'stu) – salutations to you; लंबोदराय (laṃbodarāya) – to the big-bellied (laṃb:big | udara:belly); एकदंताय (ekadantāya) – to the one-tusked (eka:one | danta:tusk); विघ्ननाशिने (vighnanāśine) – to the destroyer of obstacles (vighna:obstacle | nāśine:to the destroyer); शिवसुताय (śivasutāya) – to the son of Shiva (śiva:Shiva | suta:son); श्रीवरदमूर्तये (śrīvaradamūrtaye) – to the form bestowing desires (śrī:auspicious | varada:bestowing desires | mūrti:form); नमो (namo) – salutations; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations;

contentBlocks.original:
॥ फलश्रुति ॥

एतदथर्वशीर्षं योऽधीते ॥ स ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते ॥
स सर्वतः सुखमेधते ॥ स सर्व विघ्नैर्नबाध्यते ॥
स पंचमहापापात्प्रमुच्यते ॥
सायमधीयानो दिवसकृतं पापं नाशयति ॥
प्रातरधीयानो रात्रिकृतं पापं नाशयति ॥
सायंप्रातः प्रयुञ्जानो पापोऽपापो भवति ॥
सर्वत्राधीयानोऽपविघ्नो भवति ॥
धर्मार्थकाममोक्षं च विंदति ॥
इदमथर्वशीर्षमशिष्याय न देयम् ॥
यो यदि मोहाद्दास्यति स पापीयान् भवति
सहस्रावर्तनात् यं यं काममधीते
तं तमनेन साधयेत् ॥ ११॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
phalaśruti
etadatharvaśīrṣaṃ yo'dhīte . sa brahmabhūyāya kalpate .
sa sarvataḥ sukhamedhate . sa sarva vighnairnabādhyate .
sa pañcamahāpāpātpramucyate .
sāyamadhīyāno divasakṛtaṃ pāpaṃ nāśayati .
prātaradhīyāno rātrikṛtaṃ pāpaṃ nāśayati .
sāyaṃprātaḥ prayuñjāno pāpo'pāpo bhavati .
sarvatrādhīyāno'pavighno bhavati .
dharmārthakāmamokṣaṃ ca vindati .
idam atharvaśīrṣam aśiṣyāya na deyam .
yo yadi mohāddāsyati sa pāpīyān bhavati
sahasrāvartanāt yaṃ yaṃ kāmamadhīte
taṃ tamanena sādhayet. 11
contentBlocks.translation:
Now for the fruits from reading this hymn:
Whoever meditates on this Atharva-Sirsha (Upanishad) becomes fit for the Realisation, ‘I am the infinite Truth'. He is not affected by any of the obstacles. He gains happiness everywhere, at all times, under all circumstances. He becomes free from the five great sins.
One who chants this in the evening destroys all sins committed during the day. One who chants this in the morning destroys all sins committed during the night. The one who chants both in the morning and evening becomes sinless. One who chants everywhere (and all the time) encounters no obstacle, and gains all the four types of human accomplishments, namely religious merit (dharma), wealth (artha), desired objects (kāma) and Liberation (moksha).
This Atharva-Sirsha (Upanishad) should not be given/taught to an unqualified student. One who does so due to any temptation/delusion/attachment will incur sin.
One achieves the fulfilment of all his desires by the repetition of this (Upanishad) thousand times.
contentBlocks.meanings: फलश्रुति (phalaśruti) – fruits of recitation (phala:fruits | śruti:recitation); एतत् (etat) – this; अथर्वशीर्षं (atharvaśīrṣaṃ) – Atharva-Sirsha; यः (yaḥ) – who; अधीते (adhīte) – studies; सः (saḥ) – he; ब्रह्मभूयाय (brahmabhūyāya) – for Brahmanhood (brahma:Brahman | bhūyāya:for becoming); कल्पते (kalpate) – becomes fit; सः (saḥ) – he; सर्वतः (sarvataḥ) – everywhere; सुखम् (sukham) – happiness; एधते (edhate) – gains; सः (saḥ) – he; सर्व (sarva) – all; विघ्नैः (vighnaiḥ) – obstacles; न (na) – not; बाध्यते (bādhyate) – affected; सः (saḥ) – he; पञ्चमहापापात् (pañcamahāpāpāt) – from five great sins (pañca:five | mahā:great | pāpāt:from sins); प्रमुच्यते (pramucyate) – is freed; सायम् (sāyam) – evening; अधीयानः (adhīyānaḥ) – one who studies; दिवसकृतम् (divasakṛtam) – done during the day (divasa:day | kṛtam:done); पापम् (pāpam) – sin; नाशयति (nāśayati) – destroys; प्रातर् (prātar) – morning; अधीयानः (adhīyānaḥ) – one who studies; रात्रिकृतम् (rātrikṛtam) – done during the night (rātri:night | kṛtam:done); पापम् (pāpam) – sin; नाशयति (nāśayati) – destroys; सायंप्रातः (sāyaṃprātaḥ) – evening and morning (sāyam:evening | prātaḥ:morning); प्रयुञ्जानः (prayuñjānaḥ) – one who applies; पापः (pāpaḥ) – sinful; अपापः (apāpaḥ) – sinless; भवति (bhavati) – becomes; सर्वत्र (sarvatra) – everywhere; अधीयानः (adhīyānaḥ) – one who studies; अपविघ्नः (apavighnaḥ) – without obstacles; भवति (bhavati) – becomes; धर्मार्थकाममोक्षं (dharmārthakāmamokṣaṃ) – dharma, artha, kāma, moksha (dharma:righteousness | artha:wealth | kāma:desires | mokṣa:liberation); च (ca) – and; विन्दति (vindati) – gains; इदम् (idam) – this; अथर्वशीर्षम् (atharvaśīrṣam) – Atharva-Sirsha; अशिष्याय (aśiṣyāya) – to unqualified student; न (na) – not; देयम् (deyam) – should be given; यः (yaḥ) – who; यदि (yadi) – if; मोहात् (mohāt) – due to delusion; दास्यति (dāsyati) – gives; सः (saḥ) – he; पापीयान् (pāpīyān) – sinful; भवति (bhavati) – becomes; सहस्रावर्तनात् (sahasrāvartanāt) – by thousand repetitions (sahasra:thousand | āvartanāt:repetitions); यम् (yam) – whichever; यम् (yam) – whichever; कामम् (kāmam) – desire; अधीते (adhīte) – studies; तम् (tam) – that; तम् (tam) – that; अनेन (anena) – by this; साधयेत् (sādhayet) – achieves;

contentBlocks.original:
अनेन गणपतिमभिषिंचति स वाग्मी भवति ॥
चतुर्थ्यामनश्नन् जपति स विद्यावान् भवति ।
इत्यथर्वणवाक्यम् ॥
ब्रह्माद्यावरणं विद्यात् न बिभेति कदाचनेति ॥ १२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
anena gaṇapatimabhiṣincati sa vāgmī bhavati .
caturthyāmanaśnan japati sa vidyāvān bhavati .
ityatharvaṇavākyam .
brahmādyāvaraṇaṃ vidyāt na bibheti kadācaneti. 12
contentBlocks.translation:
One who bathes Ganapati whilst chanting this Atharva-Sirsha (Upanishad) becomes an eloquent speaker (in due course).
One who whilst fasting, repeats this Atharva-Sirsha (Upanishad), on (the auspicious day of) chaturthi (fourth day of lunar fortnight), becomes a knowledgeable person.
Thus says Sage Atharvana.
One should know Brahman/Truth and its inscrutable power (ādyā or māyā) along with its veiling power (āvarana shakti). (Thus) he does not fear at any time.
contentBlocks.meanings: अनेन (anena) – by this; गणपतिम् (gaṇapatim) – Ganapati; अभिषिंचति (abhiṣiñcati) – bathes; स (sa) – he; वाग्मी (vāgmī) – eloquent speaker; भवति (bhavati) – becomes; चतुर्थ्याम् (caturthyām) – on the fourth day; अनश्नन् (anaśnan) – fasting; जपति (japati) – repeats; स (sa) – he; विद्यावान् (vidyāvān) – knowledgeable; भवति (bhavati) – becomes; इति (iti) – thus; अथर्वणवाक्यम् (atharvaṇavākyam) – Atharvana's statement (atharvaṇa:Atharvana | vākyam:statement); ब्रह्माद्यावरणम् (brahmādyāvaraṇam) – Brahman and its veiling (brahma:Brahman | ādi:and its | āvaraṇam:veiling); विद्यात् (vidyāt) – should know; न (na) – not; बिभेति (bibheti) – fears; कदाचन (kadācana) – at any time; इति (iti) – thus;

contentBlocks.original:
यो दूर्वांकुरैर्यजति स वैश्रवणोपमो भवति ॥
यो लाजैर्यजति स यशोवान् भवति ॥ स मेधावान् भवति ॥
यो मोदकसहस्रेण यजति स वाञ्छितफलमवाप्नोति ॥
यः साज्यसमिद्भिर्यजति स सर्वं लभते स सर्वं लभते ॥ १३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yo dūrvāṅkurairyajati sa vaiśravaṇopamo bhavati .
yo lājairyajati sa yaśovān bhavati . sa medhāvān bhavati .
yo modakasahasreṇa yajati sa vāñchitaphalamavāpnoti .
yaḥ sājya samidbhiryajati sa sarvaṃ labhate sa sarvaṃ labhate. 13
contentBlocks.translation:
He who worships (Lord Ganapati) with blades of durvā grass becomes as rich as the God of wealth, Kubera.
He who worships with parched grains, becomes famous (and successful); he becomes a very intelligent person.
He who worships with a thousands modakas, gets his cherished desires fulfilled.
One who performs sacrifices by offering wood together with ghee as oblation, gets everything. He gets everything.
contentBlocks.meanings: यो (yaḥ) – who; दूर्वांकुरैः (dūrvāṅkuraiḥ) – with blades of durvā grass (dūrvā:durvā grass | aṅkuraiḥ:with blades); यजति (yajati) – worships; स (sa) – he; वैश्रवणोपमः (vaiśravaṇopamaḥ) – as rich as Kubera (vaiśravaṇa:Kubera | upamaḥ:as rich as); भवति (bhavati) – becomes; यो (yaḥ) – who; लाजैः (lājaiḥ) – with parched grains; यजति (yajati) – worships; स (sa) – he; यशोवान् (yaśovān) – famous; भवति (bhavati) – becomes; स (sa) – he; मेधावान् (medhāvān) – intelligent; भवति (bhavati) – becomes; यो (yaḥ) – who; मोदकसहस्रेण (modakasahasreṇa) – with a thousand modakas (modaka:modakas | sahasreṇa:with a thousand); यजति (yajati) – worships; स (sa) – he; वाञ्छितफलम् (vāñchitaphalam) – cherished desires (vāñchita:cherished | phalam:desires); अवाप्नोति (avāpnoti) – fulfills; यः (yaḥ) – who; साज्यसमिद्भिः (sājya-samidbhiḥ) – with ghee and wood (sājya:with ghee | samidbhiḥ:with wood); यजति (yajati) – worships; स (sa) – he; सर्वम् (sarvam) – everything; लभते (labhate) – gets; स (sa) – he; सर्वम् (sarvam) – everything; लभते (labhate) – gets;

contentBlocks.original:
अष्टौ ब्राह्मणान् सम्यग्ग्राहयित्वा
सूर्यवर्चस्वी भवति ॥
सूर्यग्रहे महानद्यां प्रतिमासंनिधौ
वा जप्त्वा सिद्धमंत्रो भवति ॥
महाविघ्नात्प्रमुच्यते ॥ महादोषात्प्रमुच्यते ॥
महापापात् प्रमुच्यते ॥
स सर्वविद्भवति स सर्वविद्भवति ॥
य एवं वेद इत्युपनिषत् ॥ १४॥
ॐ शांतिः । शांतिः ॥ शांतिः ॥।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aṣṭau brāhmaṇān samyaggrāhayitvā
sūryavarcasvī bhavati .
sūryagrahe mahānadyāṃ pratimāsannidhau
vā japtvā siddhamantro bhavati .
mahāvighnāt pramucyate . mahādoṣātpramucyate .
mahāpāpāt pramucyate . mahāpratyavāyāt pramucyate.
sa sarvavidbhavati sa sarvavidbhavati .
ya evaṃ veda ityupaniṣat. 14
oṃ śāntiḥ śāntiḥ śāntiḥ ..
contentBlocks.translation:
One who makes eight good natured (noble) persons proficient in Atharva-Sirsha (Upanishad) becomes as glorious as the Sun.
By chanting this Atharva-Sirsha (Upanishad) during solar eclipse, or by sitting on the banks of a sacred river, or by sitting in front of an idol (of Ganapati), a seeker or spiritual practitioner gets whatever that is promised in these mantras. (In particular) he becomes free from big obstacles, free from all negative tendencies and free from all sins (penalty for non-performance of obligatory duty).
He who knows in this way (the true nature of Ganapati as said in this Upanishad) knows all. He indeed knows all. Thus (concludes) the Upanishad.
oṃ śāntiḥ śāntiḥ śāntiḥ .. (May this bring Peace to all at all the three levels - Adhibhautika, Adhidaivika and Adhyatmika)
contentBlocks.meanings: अष्टौ (aṣṭau) – eight; ब्राह्मणान् (brāhmaṇān) – Brahmins; सम्यग्ग्राहयित्वा (samyaggrāhayitvā) – having made proficient (samyak:properly | grāhayitvā:having made understand); सूर्यवर्चस्वी (sūryavarcasvī) – as glorious as the Sun (sūrya:Sun | varcasvī:glorious); भवति (bhavati) – becomes; सूर्यग्रहे (sūryagrahe) – during solar eclipse (sūrya:Sun | grahe:eclipse); महानद्यां (mahānadyāṃ) – on the great river (mahā:great | nadyāṃ:on the river); प्रतिमासंनिधौ (pratimāsannidhau) – in front of an idol (pratimā:idol | sannidhau:in front of); वा (vā) – or; जप्त्वा (japtvā) – having chanted; सिद्धमंत्रो (siddhamantro) – with perfected mantra (siddha:perfected | mantraḥ:mantra); भवति (bhavati) – becomes; महाविघ्नात् (mahāvighnāt) – from great obstacles (mahā:great | vighnāt:from obstacles); प्रमुच्यते (pramucyate) – is freed; महादोषात् (mahādoṣāt) – from great faults (mahā:great | doṣāt:from faults); प्रमुच्यते (pramucyate) – is freed; महापापात् (mahāpāpāt) – from great sins (mahā:great | pāpāt:from sins); प्रमुच्यते (pramucyate) – is freed; महाप्रत्यवायात् (mahāpratyavāyāt) – from great penalties (mahā:great | pratyavāyāt:from penalties); प्रमुच्यते (pramucyate) – is freed; स (sa) – he; सर्वविद्भवति (sarvavidbhavati) – becomes all-knowing (sarva:all | vidbhavati:becomes knowing); स (sa) – he; सर्वविद्भवति (sarvavidbhavati) – becomes all-knowing (sarva:all | vidbhavati:becomes knowing); य (ya) – who; एवं (evaṃ) – thus; वेद (veda) – knows; इति (iti) – thus; उपनिषत् (upaniṣat) – Upanishad; ॐ (oṃ) – Om; शांतिः (śāntiḥ) – peace; शांतिः (śāntiḥ) – peace; शांतिः (śāntiḥ) – peace;
